dumps.notes: Program PROM hypothesis; M25A10PA plausibly corresponds to 256 kbit / 32 KB. Disassemble with ../mame/unidasm -arch upd7810 -basepc 0x0000.

dumps.expected_size: 128 KB / 1 Mbit if M10A10LA is wired like the observed LQ-500 board: pin 1 is A15, pin 20 is /CE, and pin 22 is A16/BK3
dumps.programmer_device_type: patched minipro/T48: LQ500_4C_A16_PIN22@DIP28; fallback provenance reads: 27C512@DIP28 and LQ500_4C_PIN22_HIGH@DIP28
dumps.notes: Board photos suggest 4C is a socketed ROM-like DIP28 device. Native T48 AT27C011/D27C011 reads mirrored the 27C512 capture and did not expose BK3/A16. The patched custom PROM profile LQ500_4C_A16_PIN22@DIP28 should produce a full 128 KB read; verify its low 64 KB half against the 27C512 capture and its high 64 KB half against a repeated LQ500_4C_PIN22_HIGH@DIP28 read.

dumps.expected_size: confirm from chip markings; 4M label likely means 4 Mbit if populated
dumps.notes: Character generator select; banked into 8000h-A000h. Board photos suggest this footprint may be unpopulated and labeled 4M/2M/1M/256kbit MASK IMPROM; do not assume stock population or size without chip markings.
Use for optional font module or any extra CG ROM present.
